critical_issues: No feature implementation was applied: all listed files are marked 'planned, not applied', so Gmail Trigger functionality does not exist.
No database migration was created/applied, leaving required config/state/dedup/dead-letter persistence absent.
No tests were added or executed; all required scenarios (OAuth lifecycle, dedup/checkpoint correctness, watch/poll behavior, retry/error handling) remain unverified.
Merge would create a false-positive delivery state for a high-risk trigger feature with at-least-once semantics and strict idempotency requirements.
improvements: Re-run implementation in a writable environment and submit actual code diffs for migration, Gmail adapter, worker/orchestrator, webhook, validation, and payload normalization.
Add integration and fault-injection tests for watch + poll flows, invalid historyId recovery, duplicate/out-of-order notifications, and restart safety.
Implement and verify transactional ordering between event emission and checkpoint updates, plus unique dedup keys and TTL cleanup.
Expose operational visibility (health, lag, renewal status, retry counts, paused reason) and ensure API/UI schema validation matches the planned error contract.
security_findings: Token and email-content handling safeguards are not enforceable yet because no code exists; risk remains for accidental logging of refresh tokens, bodies, or attachment bytes.
OAuth failure handling (revoked credentials, insufficient scope) is not implemented, so secure fail-closed pause/error behavior is missing.
No implemented limits/policies for attachment download/size/streaming, leaving potential data-exposure and resource-abuse paths unresolved.
performance_findings: No polling cadence, backoff, or quota-aware request strategy is implemented; quota exhaustion and burst instability risks remain unmitigated.
No dedup/checkpoint implementation exists, so duplicate processing overhead and replay amplification remain unresolved.
No attachment streaming/size enforcement exists, so memory pressure risks under large MIME payloads are unaddressed.
